risks.title: Linopy model-building overhead at production scale
risks.description: PyPSA's Linopy layer takes 302 seconds to construct the optimization model for a 10,000-bus DC OPF (C-3), compared to 6 seconds of solver time. At production scale with repeated solves, this overhead may dominate wall-clock time.
risks.mitigation: Profile Linopy model construction during Phase 2 development. If overhead is prohibitive, evaluate direct solver interface (HiGHS C API) for the inner loop, using PyPSA only for network data management and result post-processing. The solver-only time (6.2s on 10,000 buses) is competitive.

risks.title: No native PSS/E RAW file parser
risks.description: PyPSA has no PSS/E import capability (P2-1). Phase 2 requires ingesting the full network model distributed in PSS/E RAW format. A custom converter must be built or an existing parser must be adapted.
risks.mitigation: Build a PSS/E RAW-to-PyPSA converter during Phase 2. The FNM intermediate format (CSV-based) already exists and was used for Phase 1 FNM testing. Alternatively, use an external parser as a preprocessing step and ingest via the existing MATPOWER .m importer.

risks.title: Distributed slack OPF not available in optimization context
risks.description: PyPSA supports distributed slack in power flow but not in OPF formulations (A-11, partial_pass). LMP decomposition without distributed slack produces systematically different values from published data.
risks.mitigation: Implement distributed slack as a custom Linopy constraint via PyPSA's extra_functionality callback. The PTDF formulation with load-proportional weights is well-documented. Estimated effort: weeks.

risks.title: Single-threaded HiGHS solver limits parallelism
risks.description: HiGHS simplex does not parallelize; SCOPF on a 10,000-bus network showed 0.92x speedup with 32 threads (C-8). Production contingency sweeps will not benefit from multi-core hardware through solver-internal threading.
risks.mitigation: Use process-level parallelism for independent contingency sub-problems rather than relying on solver-internal threading. PyPSA's architecture supports independent model instantiation per contingency group.

risks.title: Piecewise-linear cost curves require tranche workaround
risks.description: PyPSA does not natively support piecewise-linear cost curves (P2-2, partial). The workaround (generator tranche decomposition) approximates PWL behavior but introduces modeling complexity.
risks.mitigation: Implement the generator tranche decomposition as a reusable utility during Phase 2. Validate against reference PWL solutions from a tool with native SOS2 support to ensure numerical fidelity.
